RT @sickjew: KFC doesn't pay enough? "Go to school."
School costs money? "Get a job."
Can't find job? "Work at KFC."
KFC doesn't pay enough…

@kiphillreporter Happy we agree on that.

But I think we disagree that it's responsibility of media to fix that. It's on us. Not them.
@kiphillreporter imo media needs to address that question head on- not decry students not talking to press- but to fix that relationship

Why let someone else tell your story when you can tell it yourself?
Why let media have a discourse can have yourself without them?
@kiphillreporter You see the point, though- why give up your voice and story to someone else if you can reach an audience on your own?

@kiphillreporter You mean a discourse had by individuals w/ each other (like this!) rather than appointed talking heads on talk shows?

Good

Yet I follow a lot of people on the off chance that they discover something unique. I still want their content on the periphery just in case
Right now most twitter streams are a barrage of equally-weighted tweets clamoring for attention. Interesting ones should stand out naturally
I want a Twitter client that makes incoming tweets more/less opaque depending on  how often I click their links, reply at them, or keywords.
At Max and Zack's with the fam.
Just found myself trying to think of an amusing way to kill a newborn child so I could make a joke. What has the world become?
